Title: Cloning and expression analysis of DnMSI1 gene in orchid species Dendrobium nobile Lindl

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Phylogenetic analysis shows DnMSI1 is most closely related to DcMSI1 from Dendrobium catenatum, indicating conservation of MSI1-like proteins within orchids. Functional characterization of DnMSI1 was performed in Arabidopsis as a heterologous system, but the gene itself originates from D. nobile.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. DnMSI1, an MSI1-like WD40 repeat protein with four WD40 domains, was cloned from Dendrobium nobile and shows high sequence similarity to MSI1 proteins from other plant species.
2. DnMSI1 is predominantly expressed in buds, with lower expression in stems and leaves and minimal expression in roots.
3. DnMSI1 transcription in buds is downregulated by abiotic stresses including drought, Cd, Ni, and NaCl, and is differentially regulated by plant growth regulators: induced by GA3 and TDZ and suppressed by ABA and paclobutrazol (PP333).
4. Constitutive overexpression of DnMSI1 in Arabidopsis thaliana leads to increased sensitivity to NaCl, with stronger inhibition of primary root growth under high salinity compared with WT.
5. These data support that DnMSI1 acts as a hormone-responsive, negative regulator of salinity-stress tolerance in plants, with likely functional relevance to stress responses in Dendrobium nobile.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Constitutive overexpression of DnMSI1 significantly decreases tolerance to NaCl stress, leading to stronger inhibition of primary root growth under high salinity.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Hormone-responsive abiotic stress pathway in which DnMSI1 expression is induced by GA3 and cytokinin-like TDZ and repressed by ABA and GA-biosynthesis inhibition (PP333), contributing negatively to salinity-stress responses.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: MSI1-like WD40 repeat protein containing four WD40 repeat domains.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: In D. nobile, DnMSI1 transcripts are detected in buds, stems, and leaves but hardly in roots, with highest expression in buds. In buds, DnMSI1 expression is downregulated by drought, Cd, Ni, and NaCl stress. DnMSI1 is induced by GA3 and TDZ, peaking around 10–15 days after treatment, and is repressed by ABA and the GA-biosynthesis inhibitor PP333. In Arabidopsis DnMSI1-overexpression lines, DnMSI1 transcript levels are much higher than in WT and decrease upon NaCl treatment but remain above WT levels.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Hormone and growth regulator treatments (GA3, thidiazuron/TDZ, abscisic acid/ABA, paclobutrazol/PP333), drought, CdCl2, NiCl2, and NaCl-induced salinity stress in D. nobile; NaCl (30–300 mM, including 100 and 180 mM) in hydroponic culture of Arabidopsis DnMSI1-overexpression lines.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress response and hormone-regulated development in Dendrobium nobile.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance, particularly salt stress, with additional responses to drought and heavy metal (Cd, Ni) stress.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: In D. nobile buds, DnMSI1 transcript levels decreased by 25% (drought), 19% (Cd), 22% (Ni), and 59% (NaCl) relative to control. In Arabidopsis, at 180 mM NaCl, primary root growth was inhibited by 43% in WT and by 58% and 65% in two DnMSI1-overexpressing lines.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: DnMSI1-overexpressing Arabidopsis lines show no obvious growth differences from WT under non-stress conditions. In D. nobile, DnMSI1 is strongly expressed in buds, moderately in stems and leaves, and barely detected in roots.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Dendrobium nobile plants grown in greenhouse conditions; wild-type Arabidopsis thaliana Col-0; DnMSI1-overexpressing Arabidopsis lines (AtOE-2 and AtOE-7).
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: RT-PCR and 5′/3′ RACE cloning of DnMSI1; sequence alignment and phylogenetic analysis; semi-quantitative RT-PCR and RT-qPCR to profile DnMSI1 expression in tissues and under hormone (GA3, TDZ, ABA, PP333) and abiotic stress (drought, Cd, Ni, NaCl) treatments; generation of 35S::DnMSI1 transgenic Arabidopsis lines via Agrobacterium-mediated transformation; hydroponic NaCl treatments and measurement of primary root length to calculate relative root growth.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression of DnMSI1 in Arabidopsis thaliana (35S::DnMSI1) combined with NaCl stress assays measuring relative root growth and RT-qPCR analysis of DnMSI1 transcript levels.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Homology-based cloning from D. nobile buds using RT-PCR with primers designed from conserved Nicotiana tabacum MSI1 motifs, followed by 5′ and 3′ RACE to obtain the full-length cDNA.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: DnMSI1 is a hormone- and stress-responsive regulator whose overexpression decreases plant tolerance to salinity stress, suggesting that down-regulation or loss-of-function of DnMSI1 could be used to enhance salt-stress resistance and improve growth performance of ornamental and medicinal Dendrobium nobile under saline or other abiotic stress conditions.
